data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T003905-2d44d34b/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T003905-2d44d34b/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T003905-2d44d34b/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T003905-2d44d34b/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T003905-2d44d34b/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-051500ff">
    <title>bead: auto-commit attachment writes from execute-loop and migrate</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Two code paths write to .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl as part of normal operation, and neither auto-commits the result. Operators end up with persistent untracked-and-modified worktree state that contradicts the project's intent (1083 attachment files ARE tracked across 11.6 MB of committed history — this is first-class audit-trail data, not detritus).

Concrete demonstration today:

1. Run `ddx try <id>` on 9 beads → 9 new untracked .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/ dirs appear in main repo. Each contains routing decisions, cost-per-attempt, execution-routing-intent, prose-check.findings, execute-bead outcome — exactly the audit data the convention says we keep.
2. Run `ddx bead migrate` → 142 events externalized into existing tracked attachment files (showing as M), 1 new untracked attachment dir, 292 beads archived (modifying .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl), and a fresh worktree of 151 dirty files. None committed.
3. The audit log clearly shows the historical pattern: commits like `878931ce3 chore(beads): commit missing event attachments` and `5d54258fd Merge bead ... attempt ... into main` periodically sweep up the accumulation — but it's recurring manual cleanup, not the steady-state design.

ROOT CAUSE
Two distinct writers, neither stages or commits its output:

A. The execute-loop emits per-attempt routing/cost/intent events. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go emitRouteFailureEvent (line 3872) and the routing/cost emitters all call store.AppendEvent → the bead store writes to .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl when the bead's events are externalized (or to the inline events array when not). The Store.AppendEvent path has no follow-up `git add`. The execute-loop's chore("add execution evidence") commit (visible in git log as `chore: add execution evidence [<run-id>]`) stages `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` per AGENTS.md convention — but NOT `.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/`.

B. cli/internal/bead/migrate.go ArchiveWithEvents (line 561) externalizes inline events from closed/archived beads to attachments. After externalizing 142 events today across many files, the migrate command exits without staging or committing the resulting changes. The operator command surface (cli/cmd/bead.go migrate handler) has no auto-commit step analogous to the bead store's other auto-commit paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Execute-loop landing path: extend the existing post-merge "chore(beads): commit missing event attachments"-style commit (or the bead's own evidence commit) to ALSO stage .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/ for the bead just landed. Use the same git-index-lock recovery (cli/internal/gitlock per ddx-6a518fd9) so concurrent workers and the statusline poller do not race.
2. `ddx bead migrate` auto-commit: after a successful migrate that wrote any attachment changes (newly externalized + newly archived rows), call a single auto-commit at end-of-run with message `chore(beads): externalize and archive (<N> events to <M> attachments, <K> beads archived)`. Skip the commit entirely when no changes were written (idempotent re-run is a no-op).
3. Regression invariant: after `ddx try <id>` completes with `success`/`already_satisfied`, `git status --short` in the main repo MUST show zero untracked files under `.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/` for that bead.
4. Operator escape hatch: `ddx bead reconcile-attachments` — single command that stages every untracked .ddx/attachments/ dir and any modified attachment files, commits as `chore(beads): reconcile missing attachment commits (<N> dirs, <M> modified)`. Useful for one-shot cleanup of pre-existing accumulation when this fix lands (today's 10 untracked + 141 modified dirs are an immediate use case).

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change WHAT gets externalized (the migrate event-externalization logic is fine).
- Do not change ATTACHMENT FORMAT or location.
- Do not add attachments to .gitignore (they ARE valuable cross-machine audit data per characterization).
- Do not auto-commit .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ — that's per-machine and stays untracked under existing convention.

DEPS
ddx-6a518fd9 (landed) — required so the auto-commits route through index-lock recovery and don't bounce on operator/statusline git contention.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestExecuteLoopLanding_StagesAttachmentDir asserts that after a `ddx try <id>` returns success, the .ddx/attachments/<id>/ dir for that bead is committed (`git ls-files .ddx/attachments/<id>/` returns the events.jsonl, and `git status --short` shows no untracked entries under that path).
2. TestExecuteLoopLanding_AttachmentsCommittedUnderIndexLockContention asserts the auto-commit succeeds even when a stale .git/index.lock is present at commit time (regression guard for the gitlock recovery integration).
3. TestBeadMigrate_AutoCommits asserts that `ddx bead migrate` against a repo with closed beads carrying inline events writes the attachments AND creates a single commit with message matching `^chore\(beads\): externalize and archive`. The commit's path list includes all modified attachment files and any new attachment dirs.
4. TestBeadMigrate_NoChangesNoCommit asserts `ddx bead migrate` against an already-migrated repo (no inline events to externalize, no beads to archive) does NOT create an empty commit.
5. TestBeadReconcileAttachments asserts the new `ddx bead reconcile-attachments` operator command stages and commits any untracked .ddx/attachments/ dirs and modified attachment files, and prints a summary line `chore(beads): reconcile missing attachment commits (<N> dirs, <M> modified)`.
6. TestBeadReconcileAttachments_NoChanges asserts the same command in a clean repo prints "no attachment changes to reconcile" and exits 0 without committing.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:bead, area:execution, area:devx, kind:bug, phase:2</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="118811243c0cce2a8bb0f6c9515af2dbf8547ce1" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T003905-2d44d34b"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

candidates.reason: provider bragi known unreachable (last dial failure 3s ago)
model "anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5" has no catalog power metadata

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

offset.description: 0-based line offset to start reading from

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return

tools.name: write
tools.description: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Creates parent directories as needed.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

oldText.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file and must not overlap with other edits)

old_string.description: Legacy: exact string to find (use edits[] instead)

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Execute a shell command for targeted repo operations such as builds, tests, or git. Do not use this for file discovery or file reading; use the find, grep, ls, and read tools instead. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.

tools.name: find
tools.description: Find files by path pattern. Use this instead of shell find or ls when locating files by name.

pattern.description: Path pattern to match (e.g. '**/*.go', 'cmd/**/main.go'). Use ** to match across directories.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative or absolute; defaults to working directory)

glob.description: Restrict to files whose base names match this glob (e.g. '*.go', '*.ts')

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: ls
tools.description: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.

path.description: Directory to list (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

tools.name: patch
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file. Supports: replace (default), replace_all, prepend, and append operations.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

search.description: Exact text to find in the file. Must match exactly (including whitespace). Will match first occurrence.

content.description: Replacement text, or text to prepend/append

operation.description: One of: 'replace' (default, search must be unique), 'replace_all' (replace every occurrence), 'prepend' (insert at file start), 'append' (insert at file end)

tools.name: task
tools.description: Track subtasks in a multi-step plan: create, update, get, list.

id.description: Task ID (required for update/get, ignored for create/list)
